Cameron Drinkwater, Chief Product and Operations Officer at S&P Dow Jones Indices, noted, “This partnership expands the accessibility and usability of our flagship benchmark indices in the digital trading environment. We believe that digital-native investors should demand the institutional-grade quality standards that define our indices, and we are excited to partner with Trade[XYZ] to achieve this.” III. Other Recent Developments with Hyperliquid 1. Crypto Traders Turn to Hyperliquid for Oil Trading Cryptocurrency traders are increasingly using Hyperliquid to trade oil, with the 24/7 crypto market absorbing trades related to global macroeconomic shocks. On Wednesday, a team of analysts at JPMorgan Chase noted that earlier this month, as the war with Iran escalated over the weekend, trading volume surged on the Hyperliquid platform for oil-related perpetual futures contracts—West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil futures contracts (CL-USDC)—while traditional exchanges such as the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) were not operating. Analysts say geopolitical shocks may continue to drive intermittent surges in trading on 24/7 cryptocurrency trading platforms as traders try to gain an early advantage before global events occur. If this dynamic persists, platforms like Hyperliquid could become early channels for traders to price global risks before traditional markets. 2. Whales Building Positions in HYPE According to Onchain Lens monitoring, bullish whales are concentrating their positions in HYPE through the Hyperliquid platform. Whale "0x96d" deposited 4.44 million USDC, increasing its HYPE holdings to 253,442 tokens (worth $10.55 million), at an average price of $32.57, with $1.3 million in buy orders pending. Whale "0x780" deposited 2.67 million USDC and bought 63,547 HYPE tokens at $42.06. Whale "0x50b" spent $1.98 million to buy 47,076 HYPE tokens at $42.14. The total investment exceeded $9 million. Currently, the market is generally optimistic about HYPE's future price performance. In a Polymarket prediction regarding "How high will the price of Hyperliquid reach in March?", 45% of participants believe it will reach above $44.

Appendix: S&P's Crypto Ecosystem Path

  • May 2021: S&P Dow Jones Indices launched its first cryptocurrency index series, the "S&P Digital Markets Indexes," which includes Bitcoin and Ethereum indices.

  • May 2022: A dedicated DeFi team was established, led by Chuck Mounts, Chief DeFi Officer of S&P Global.

  • May 2022: A dedicated DeFi team was established, led by Chuck Mounts, Chief DeFi Officer of S&P Global.

    • January 2023: Launch of the S&P Crypto DeFi Index, a cryptocurrency benchmark focused on DeFi that tracks the performance of selected DeFi tokens/protocols. December 2023: S&P Global Ratings launches a stablecoin stability assessment framework covering leading stablecoins. February to September 2025: S&P Global Ratings rates Janus Henderson's Anemoy Tokenized Treasury Fund, Delta Wellington's Ultra Short Treasury On-Chain Fund, and OpenEden Group Ltd.'s Tokenized TBILL Fund for the first time. July 2025: S&P DJI partners with Centrifuge to enter the fund tokenization space by gaining authorization from the S&P 500 Index. August 2025: DeFi protocol receives its first credit rating (Sky Protocol, B- Stable Outlook). September 2025: Centrifuge launches SPXA, the first permissioned S&P 500 index fund token. October 2025: S&P DJI announces plans to launch the S&P Digital Market 50 Index, an innovative crypto ecosystem index combining cryptocurrencies and crypto-related stocks. October 2025: S&P Global Ratings puts its SSA on-chain via Chainlink. February 2026: S&P Global Ratings releases its first-ever rating for a Bitcoin-backed structured finance transaction.

data.title: After the stablecoin wave, who will be the next asset to be tokenized?
data.description:
Author: mariaa.eth, General Partner & Translation: Saoirse, Foresight News

Abstract

We have compiled 501 real-world revenue streams and cross-referenced them with currently implemented Real-World Assets (RWAs) on-chain.

  • Stablecoin demand first brought government bonds onto the blockchain. Now, the high concentration of government bond assets is further driving higher-yield assets onto the blockchain.

  • Mainstream solutions that make high-yield assets appear "instantly liquidated" will ultimately pull yields towards the risk-free rate.

  • Tokenizable revenue streams are extremely abundant. Seven opportunity segments reveal the value space that can be unlocked on-chain.

  • The biggest challenge lies in distribution. Of the 35 non-stablecoin real-world assets with a market capitalization exceeding $50 million and generating returns, only two have more than 2,000 holders. This is largely due to the current mechanism design. Despite these obstacles, on-chain real-world assets will continue to grow. Two-thirds of stablecoin reserves existed long before the advent of on-chain risk-free rates; now, the total stablecoin market capitalization has surpassed $280 billion, and structural demand is driving more real-world assets onto the blockchain. Entrepreneurs have the opportunity to bring assets that are difficult to tokenize onto the blockchain, control their distribution channels, or build the underlying infrastructure capable of supporting all assets. Demand for stablecoins is driving the on-chaining of government bonds, and increased concentration is propelling high-yield assets to follow suit. Previously, the supply of stablecoins had an inverse relationship with the federal funds rate: when the rate was near zero, the supply exceeded $180 billion; when the rate climbed above 5%, the supply declined. However, this pattern was broken in January 2024—even with interest rates remaining above 5%, the stablecoin supply began to grow again, and has now exceeded $280 billion. The supply of stablecoins moved inversely to the federal funds rate until January 2024, after which the two began to decouple. (Data source: Electric Capital Stablecoin Dashboard, Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED)). The core change is that the risk-free rate has officially appeared on-chain. Starting with Ondo in 2023, and followed by BlackRock and Centrifuge in 2024, issuers began offering Treasury yields in the form of on-chain tokens. For the first time, stablecoin holders can obtain risk-free returns without leaving the crypto ecosystem.

    Interest-bearing real-world assets (RWAs) categorized by launch date. Bubble size reflects total asset value. The largest assets are concentrated in the 3-5% yield range. Newer, smaller products have pushed up the yield curve. Data source: 7-day trailing APY and launch date from rwa.xyz, DeFiLlama Yields API, protocol documentation.

    Government bonds are now the largest real-world asset class, with a size of approximately $11 billion.

    The same logic has also driven other debt-type assets onto the blockchain: Private credit: $2.8 billion; Corporate bonds: $1.9 billion; Non-US sovereign debt: $1.1 billion. The market is highly concentrated: the top 10 assets account for 64% of the total value, and the yields of the top 18 products are concentrated between 3% and 5%. This concentration is forcing stablecoin reserves to diversify, thereby driving higher-yield assets onto the blockchain. However, the difficulty of blockchaining high-yield assets is far greater than that of government bonds. All tokenized assets suffer from time mismatch, and all compromises diminish returns. On-chain funds flow 24/7, settle in seconds, and can be reconfigured within the same block; off-chain assets cannot do this. Every tokenization of a real-world asset faces this time mismatch, primarily manifested in two aspects: **Configuration Lag:** Funds deposited on-chain only begin to generate returns after being actually invested in the underlying assets. A private loan may require weeks for approval, and a real estate transaction may take months to complete. Before that, the funds are in a state of idleness. **Redemption Lag:** When investors want to withdraw, the underlying assets cannot be immediately liquidated. Even BUIDL, the largest tokenized government bond product, requires daily settlement through BlackRock. It's only because Circle pre-funds USDC that users experience the feeling of "instant arrival." ACRED's underlying fund only opens for redemption once a quarter; real estate funds are locked for years. Government bonds, already the most liquid off-chain assets, still require additional solutions to achieve "on-chain immediacy," and this is even more true for high-yield assets. The cost of a solution is directly proportional to the illiquidity of the asset: the slower and less liquid the underlying asset, the more returns the compromise solution consumes. Currently, there are three main solutions to bridge the time gap, all essentially transferring the illiquidity cost to the willing party: A. Placing idle funds in highly liquid assets. A portion of the fund pool is placed in low-yield, but readily available assets, allowing new funds to immediately generate returns and withdrawals to proceed without waiting for the underlying asset to mature.

    • Putting into DeFi lending: For example, Maple's syrupUSD pool, placing uninvested funds in protocols like Aave as a buffer, new funds immediately accrue interest, but this lowers the overall yield.

    • Holding short-term government bonds as a buffer: For example, sUSDai uses government bonds as the base yield while issuing loans secured by GPUs.

    B. Distributing yield within a liquidity pool

    Directly merging new funds into a liquidity pool that is already generating yield, so that individual users do not feel the allocation delay. The cost is: existing users subsidize new users, and the larger the liquidity pool, the less obvious the dilution effect. A typical example is Morpho Vault.

Full text: Complete dataset of 501 revenue sources, interactive charts, available by clicking the link.

The biggest challenge: distribution

New revenue types and new investment strategies only make sense when they reach the funds.

Opportunities Across the Entire Industry Chain

  • Discovering and Tokenizing New Asset Categories

  • Building Infrastructure to Make Assets Easier to Go On-Chain

  • Designing Fund Allocation and Redemption Schemes That Don't Deplete Returns

  • Providing On-Chain Return Exposure with Synthetic Products Before the Underlying Assets Are Fully Tokenized

  • Breaking the Current Distribution Pattern Reliant on Large Institutions and Opening Up New Institutional Funding Channels

  • Structured Design for Each Type of RWA to Cover More Types of Users

Every new asset going on-chain makes it easier for the next asset to go on-chain, and the infrastructure supporting it will also become more valuable.

Every new asset going on-chain makes it easier for the next asset to go on-chain, and the infrastructure supporting it will also become more valuable.

Research Methodology We analyzed 15 asset classes and 501 independent sources of income, excluding 34 types that relied on non-transferable legal relationships, tax incentives in specific jurisdictions, or had no active market. Ultimately, 467 types were used as the analysis sample. On-chain Status Criteria: Tokenized: At least one corresponding on-chain product with a scale ≥ $50 million; Partially Tokenized: On-chain products exist, but all are < $50 million; Non-Tokenized: No on-chain products currently exist. Gold, silver, private equity, and other assets that do not generate stable returns are not included in the income source statistics; stocks are only classified as partially tokenized because the on-chain recording of dividends has not yet been automated. All data is as of March 8, 2026.

data.title: Why is Stripe starting anew? Will it threaten the future of the x402?
data.description:
Author: Haotian; Source: X, @tmel0211

Two days ago, we were talking about how the x402 protocol is becoming increasingly mainstream, after all, AWS has released a report touting it, Circle's financial report also shows it, and Mastercard, Alchemy, and others are supporting it. Then, WTF, we saw the MPP (Machine Payments Protocol) incubated by the Tempo public chain.

The question is, why is Stripe starting from scratch? Will this threaten the future of the x402 protocol?

There are two main differences:

1) The underlying logic of x402 focuses on the on-chain settlement logic of "per request". The engineering implementation will inevitably have greater on-chain friction, and it will be extremely sensitive to the chain's native concurrency and small fee rates.

MPP introduces a Sessions feature, where the Agent only needs to perform a handshake authentication and set a spending limit once. Hundreds or even thousands of high-frequency API calls are then packaged and settled later. To put it simply, MPP is like an extended L2 protocol layer of x402, using high concurrency to overcome the cumbersome nature of pure on-chain solutions. 2) MPP adopts a hybrid approach of fiat currency and crypto assets, positioning itself as a "rail-agnostic" general-purpose extension layer. Its advantages are also obvious: Stripe's existing millions of merchants provide a strong initial advantage, and its closer proximity to fiat currency payment channels allows for rapid short-term channel coverage and data volume growth. x402, however, is quite different. Embracing native stablecoins, its inherent advantages—a permissionless and chain-agnostic open nature—make it more likely to become a truly cross-ecosystem underlying payment protocol in the future. Currently, native chains and ecosystems such as Ethereum, Base, and Solana are already fully integrating, and the foundation of the A2A economy is based on x402 as its core payment channel. Therefore, x402 is more representative of the future lifeline of the Crypto economy. McKinsey recently predicted that the Agent payments market will reach $3-5 trillion by 2030. Before this massive incremental market arrives, traditional payment giants will certainly not be content to remain merely an underlying channel within the x402 ecosystem. Therefore, with Stripe entering the fray, it's possible that Visa and others will follow suit, vying for market share, much like the chaotic battles between Tether, Cricket, and Stripe in the stablecoin public blockchain arena. However, in my view, the outcome of this battle was already decided from the start. x402 may face short-term threats, given the obvious channel advantages of MPP's cold start, but looking at the long term, x402 represents true HTTP-level underlying protocol innovation and will be the future decentralized payment infrastructure for TradeFi + DeFi integration; its position will never be shaken.

data.title: Coinbase Commerce Exit Turns Risky as Users Directed to Enter Sensitive Recovery Phrases on Official Subdomain
data.description:
The Digital Key Left Under The Mat

The cardinal rule of cryptocurrency is simple: never share your 12-word seed phrase.

This string of words acts as the master key to a user's entire digital fortune, and every security guide in the industry warns that entering these words into a website is the fastest way to lose everything.

However, as Coinbase prepares to shutter its Commerce product by 31 March 2026, the company has introduced a recovery tool that asks users to do exactly that.

By hosting a web form that prompts merchants to type their plaintext mnemonic phrases directly into a browser, the exchange has created a security dilemma that experts say contradicts years of basic safety education.

Why Are Security Experts Raising The Alarm

The controversy gained momentum after SlowMist founder Yu Xian, known as Cos, flagged the subdomain.

He expressed total disbelief that a major platform would facilitate such a risky workflow.

Yu Xian posted on X,

“I’m really puzzled why Coinbase would have a page like this, directly asking users to input their plaintext mnemonic phrases for asset recovery.”

He calls the practice “simply unbelievable.”

Source: X

The concern is that by making this a legitimate step in their own migration process, Coinbase is teaching users that pasting a seed phrase into a website is acceptable.

This normalisation of risky behaviour plays directly into the hands of scammers who use identical tactics to drain wallets.

Is This A Gift To Social Engineering Scammers

On-chain investigator ZachXBT was quick to point out that the existence of an official Coinbase page asking for seed phrases provides the perfect cover for criminals.

He questioned the logic behind the move, asking,

“So basically Coinbase has an official page live threat actors can use to target Coinbase users via seed phrase social engineering if they wanted?”

The danger is heightened by the looming 31 March 2026 deadline.

Source: X

With tens of thousands of merchants rushing to move funds before the platform is retired, their psychological guard is lowered.

If an attacker sends a link to a fake version of this page, a merchant might enter their keys without a second thought, believing they are simply following official Coinbase instructions.

Can This Tool Be Easily Cloned By Attackers

The technical flaws extend beyond the input field itself.

SlowMist’s Chief Information Security Officer, 23pds, noted that the site’s structure is surprisingly easy to copy.

Using basic software like ResourcesSaver, a hacker can download the front-end code and host a visually perfect replica on a lookalike domain.

Because the real Coinbase tool even suggests that users sign into Google Drive to copy and paste their phrases, the workflow encourages multiple layers of digital exposure.

Source: X

If a user has a compromised browser extension or is on an unsecure network, those 12 words are intercepted the moment they are typed, leading to an irreversible loss of funds.

How Can Merchants Protect Their Assets Safely

While Coinbase has told media outlets it is looking into the matter, it has yet to issue a wide-reaching public statement to address the specific criticisms from researchers.

However, as of writing, the page has now been disabled.

For business owners with funds still tied to the old Commerce platform, the safest route is to avoid the web-based seed phrase input entirely.

Experts suggest importing the recovery phrase directly into a trusted, standalone application like Coinbase Wallet or MetaMask on a secure device, rather than through a browser-based form.

This ensures the master key never touches a web interface, maintaining the wall between a user's life savings and the open internet.

What Are The Stakes For Coinbase Reputation

This incident comes at a difficult time for the exchange, which is currently consolidating its services under the Coinbase Business banner.

The company has previously been very vocal about security, even warning users that scammers often pose as support agents to steal login data.

In December 2025, a support impersonation scam already cost users $2 million, proving how effective these branded attacks can be.

If a mass phishing event occurs because users were following a path created by the exchange itself, the reputational damage to the industry's most prominent public company would be immense.

With only days left until the final shutdown, the community is waiting to see if the tool will be replaced with a more secure alternative.
